m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following statements are true?

Options.OptionText: Constructors cannot have a visibility modifier
Constructors can be marked public and protected, but not private
Constructors can only have a primitive return type

m_Item1.Explanation:
When a class inherits from a parent class, it does not inherit constructors.
Each class is responsible for defining its own constructors to handle object creation appropriately.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is the output?
    public class Trial
    {
            int num=10;
            void change(Trial ref)   {
                ref.num=20;
                ref=new Trial();
                ref.num=30;
                ref=null;
        }
        public static void main(String args[]) {
                Trial t=new Trial();
                t.change(t);
            System.out.println(t.num);
        }
}
m_Item1.Explanation:
t.change(t):
Initially, t.num is 10.
change(t) modifies t.num to 20.
Creates a new Trial object and assigns it to ref.
Modifies ref.num (which is now 30), but this does not affect t.
Sets ref to null, which has no effect on t.
System.out.println(t.num):
Prints the value of t.num, which was modified to 20 within the change method.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
class Bar { }
    class Test {
             Bar doBar()   {
                     Bar b = new Bar(); /* Line 6 */
                     return b; /* Line 7 */
             }
             public static void main (String args[])  {
                     Test t = new Test(); /* Line 11 */
                     Bar newBar = t.doBar(); /* Line 12 */
                     System.out.println("newBar");
                     newBar = new Bar(); /* Line 14 */
                     System.out.println("finishing"); /* Line 15 */
             }
    }
At what point is the Bar object, created on line 6, eligible for garbage collection?
m_Item1.Explanation: The Bar object created on line 6 (Bar b = new Bar();) becomes eligible for garbage collection immediately after line 14 (newBar = new Bar();) in the main method, because it is no longer referenced by any active variable.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How restrictive is the default accessibility compared to public, protected and private accessibility?

Options.OptionText: More restrictive than public,but less restrictive than protected
More restrictive than protected,but less restrictive than private

m_Item1.Explanation:
Public: The member (class, method, field) is accessible from any other class.
Protected: The member is accessible within its own package and by subclasses (even if they are in different packages).
Default (Package-private): The member is accessible only within its own package. This is the default if no access modifier is specified.
Private: The member is accessible only within its own class.
Therefore, default accessibility is more restrictive than protected because it does not allow access to subclasses outside the package, but it is less restrictive than private because it allows access within the entire package, not just within the class itself.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
A method within a class is only accessible by classes that are defined within the same package as the
class of the method. How can such a restriction be enforced?

Options.OptionText: Declare the method with the keyword “public”
Declare the method with the keyword “protected”
Do not declare the method with any modifiers.
Declare the method with the keyword “private”

m_Item1.Explanation:
C. Do not declare the method with any modifiers.
This ensures that the method has default (package-private) access, restricting access to only classes within the same package.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is garbage collection process in java?

Options.OptionText: The operating system periodically deletes all the java files available on the system.
Unused package in program is automatically deleted.

When all references to an object are gone, memory used by that object is automatically
reclaimed.
The JVM checks the output of any java program and deletes anything that does not make sense.

m_Item1.Explanation:
Garbage collection in Java is the process by which the JVM automatically manages memory by reclaiming unused objects.
Java uses automatic garbage collection to free up memory occupied by objects that are no longer referenced by any part of the program.
The JVM periodically identifies and deletes objects that are no longer reachable, freeing their memory resources for reuse.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Garbage Collection can be controlled by program?
m_Item1.Explanation: Garbage Collection cannot be controlled by program.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is process of defining two or more methods within same class that have same name but different parameters declaration?
m_Item1.Explanation: Two or more methods can have same name as long as their parameters declaration is different, the methods are said to be overloaded and process is called method overloading. Method overloading is a way by which Java implements polymorphism.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What allows the programmer to destroy an object x?

Options.OptionText: Only the garbage collection system can destroy an object.

m_Item1.Explanation: Java garbage collection is the process by which Java programs performs automatic memory management i.e. unreferenced objects are destroyed(means memory is reclaimed). Destroying x is only under the control of garbage collector.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the return type of Constructors?
m_Item1.Explanation: Constructors does not have any return type, not even void.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which keyword is used by method to refer to the object that invoked it?
m_Item1.Explanation: this keyword can be used inside any method to refer to the current object. this is always a reference to the object on which the method was invoked. .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which operator is used by Java run time implementations to free the memory of an object when it is no longer needed?
m_Item1.Explanation: Java handles deallocation of memory automatically, we do not need to explicitly delete an element. Garbage collection only occurs during execution of the program. When no references to the object exist, that object is assumed to be no longer needed, and the memory occupied by the object can be reclaimed.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following statements are incorrect?

Options.OptionText: Default constructor is called at the time of object creation
Finalize() method is called when a object goes out of scope and is no longer needed
Finalize() method must be declared private

m_Item1.Explanation: Finalize() method is called just prior to garbage collection. it is not called when object goes out of scope. .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Where is a new object allotted memory?

Options.OptionText: Young or Old space depending on space availability

m_Item1.Explanation:
The heap is divided into two generations: the young generation and the old generation. New objects are always allocated in the young generation. When the young generation becomes full, a minor garbage collection is performed. A minor garbage collection collects objects that are no longer needed from the young generation.

Objects that survive a minor garbage collection are moved to the old generation. Objects in the old generation are only collected when the old generation becomes full. A major garbage collection is performed when the old generation becomes full. A major garbage collection collects objects that are no longer needed from the old generation.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a garbage collection technique?
m_Item1.Explanation: A mark and sweep garbage collection consists of two phases, the mark phase and the sweep phase.I mark phase all the objects reachable by java threads, native handles and other root sources are marked alive and others are garbage. In sweep phase, the heap is traversed to find gaps between live objects and the gaps are marked free list used for allocating memory to new objects.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which class members can be accessed from the classes in the same package as well as classes in other Packages that are subclasses of the declaring class?
m_Item1.Explanation: Protected members are the members which are  accessible by subclasses in any package.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which variables are created when an object is created with the use of the keyword 'new' and destroyed when the object is destroyed?
m_Item1.Explanation: Instance variables get memory at the time of creating object using new and destroyed when object is garbage collected.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which function is used to perform some action when the object is to be destroyed?
m_Item1.Explanation:
The java.lang.Object.finalize() is called by the garbage collector on an object when garbage collection determines that there are no more references to the
object. A subclass overrides the finalize method to dispose of system resources or to perform other cleanup.

Following is the declaration for java.lang.Object.finalize() method
protected void finalize()

Example::
import java.util.*;

public class ObjectDemo extends GregorianCalendar {

   public static void main(String[] args) {
      try {
         // create a new ObjectDemo object
         ObjectDemo cal = new ObjectDemo();

         // print current time
         System.out.println("" + cal.getTime());

         // finalize cal
         System.out.println("Finalizing...");
         cal.finalize();
         System.out.println("Finalized.");

      } catch (Throwable ex) {
         ex.printStackTrace();
      }
   }
}

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider the following program:
class prob1 {
int puzzel(int n) {
int result;
if (n==1)
return 1;
result = puzzel(n-1) * n;
return result;
}
}
class prob2 {
public static void main(String args[]) {
prob1 f = new prob1();
System.out.println(" puzzel of 6 is = " + f.puzzel(6));
}
}
Which of the following will be the output of the above program ?
m_Item1.Explanation: puzzel() method actually calculates factorial of given number and returns using recursion. Hence the value of 6 gets displayed as 720.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is the functionality of the following piece of Java code?
Assume: ‘a’ is a non empty array of integers, the Stack class creates an array of specified size and provides a top pointer 

indicating TOS(top of stack), push and pop have normal meaning.
public void some_function(int[] a) {
Stack S=new Stack(a.length);
int[] b=new int[a.length];
for(int i=0;i<a.length;i++) {
S.push(a[i]);
}
for(int i=0;i<a.length;i++) {
b[i]=(int)(S.pop());
}
System.out.println("output :");
for(int i=0;i<b.length;i++) {
System.out.println(b[i]);
}
}
m_Item1.Explanation: Lastly inserted element of stack gets added as first element in the array. So it will reverse the orginal array a.

m_Item1.Explanation: When a boolean variable is declared as instance variable, it will be by default initialized with false. But when it is declared as loal variable, it does not have any default value, hence It must be initialized.

When //noMoreMemoryAllocation is reached, how many CardBoard objects are eligible for garbage collection?

Options.OptionText: It is not possible to know

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is garbage collection in the context of Java?

Options.OptionText: The operating system periodically deletes all of the java files available on the system.
Any package imported in a program and not used is automatically deleted
When all references to an object are gone, the memory used by the object is automatically reclaimed.
The JVM checks the output of any Java program and deletes anything that doesn’t make sense

m_Item1.Explanation: The task of the garbage collector is to reclaim the memory of unreferenced objects

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What will happen when you try to compile and run the following program? 
class Car { 
int milesPerGallon; 
int index; 
Car(int mpg) { 
milesPerGallon = mpg; 
index = 0;
} 
Car() { 
} 
public static void main(String[] args) { 
int index; 
Car c = new Car(25); 
if (args.length > 0) 
if (args[index].equals("Hiway")) 
milesPerGallon*= 2; System.out.println("mpg: " + milesPerGallon);
}
}

Options.OptionText: The code compiles and displays "mpg: 50" if the command-line argument is "Hiway". If the command-line argument is not "Hiway", the code displays "mpg: 25".
The code compiles and displays "mpg: 50" if the command-line argument is "Hiway". If the command-line argument is not "Hiway", the code throws an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
The code does not compile because the automatic variable named index has not been initialized.
The code does not compile because milesPerGallon has not been initialized.

m_Item1.Explanation: In java local variables should be intialized before they are used otherwise it is a compilation error. varibale index is not assigned with a value.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is the result of executing the following code: 
class Test { 
   public static void main(String[] args) { 
   Test t = new Test(); 
   t.test(1.0, 2L, 3);
   }  
  void test(double a, double b, short c) { 
   System.out.println('1');
  } 
  void test(float a, byte b, byte c) { 
   System.out.println('2');
  }  
  void test(double a, double b, double c) { 
           System.out.println('3');
  } 
  void test(int a, long b, int c) {
       System.out.println('4');
  } 
  void test(long a, long b, long c) { 
       System.out.println('5');
  }
}
m_Item1.Explanation: There is no test() method which accepts double, long and int as arguments. So second and third argument is implicitly promoted to higher data type i.e. double. So it will call test(double,double,double) method and print '3' on the console. Values can not be implicitly demoted to lower data types

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What will appear in the standard output when you run the Tester class? 
class Tester { 
int var; 
Tester(double var) { 
this.var = (int)var;
} 
Tester(int var) { 
this("hello");
} 
Tester(String s) { 
this(); 
System.out.println(s);
} 
Tester() { 
System.out.println("good-bye");
} 
public static void main(String[] args) { 
Tester t = new Tester(5);
}
}
m_Item1.Explanation:
In the constructor of Tester(int var), it gives call to another constructor Tester(String s) using this("hello") which in turn gives call to default constructor using this() which prints "good-bye" then "hello" gets printed from Tester(String s) constructor.
So final outcome is "good-bye" followed by "hello".
